title: China-to-Pakistan Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Pakistan 50 Hz Grid Profile and Commissioning Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Pakistan is a 50 Hz grid application, but an inverter nameplate or China factory profile does not prove that the installed unit uses the approved Pakistan protection settings. The file should document nominal frequency, voltage and frequency trip windows, disconnection times, reconnection delay, ramp-rate or export-limiting controls where used, firmware profile, lock method, screenshots, and commissioning sign-off.
target_standards: NEPRA grid-connectivity and prosumer route
Relevant DISCO commissioning and interconnection settings
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter reports are typically based on 50 Hz operation and may include reconnect, ride-through, anti-islanding, and power-quality data. Those reports are useful capability evidence, but the Pakistan project still needs local setting confirmation and site commissioning records.
rows.gap: Keep factory 50 Hz evidence and the Pakistan site record together: approved settings table, firmware version and region profile, password or remote-change control, anti-islanding evidence, meter or shutdown checks, reconnect test, installer declaration, and customer handover.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where DISCO approval, NEPRA route, project documents, or commissioning authority specifies grid settings or site evidence
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz reports and profiles are supporting evidence only unless the Pakistan project accepts them after local review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat 50 Hz sameness as a starting point; Pakistan acceptance depends on the commissioned local grid profile and records.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 37408 and GB/T 19964-2024 Baseline Evidence
rows.target_requirement: A Pakistan package should convert China inverter evidence into Pakistan-facing evidence for PSQCA scope, IEC 62109 safety, NEPRA / DISCO grid connection, 50 Hz settings, EMC, labels, manuals, commissioning, and project handover. China GB/T evidence is baseline support, not Pakistan approval.
target_standards: PSQCA / Pakistan Standard scope where specifically applicable
NEPRA / DISCO prosumer, grid-connectivity, or project route
IEC 62109 and IEC / CISPR evidence where requested
rows.china_equivalent: SAMR lists GB/T 19964-2024 as the current recommended national standard for technical requirements for connecting photovoltaic power stations to power systems, with GB/T 19964-2012 withdrawn. GB/T 37408-2019 is the current China baseline edition used here; if a lab report used a different edition, state the tested edition separately.
rows.gap: Build a clause matrix from GB/T 19964-2024 and GB/T 37408-2019 report evidence to Pakistan deliverables, showing which items are technical capability evidence and which still require Pakistan route confirmation.

mandatory_status.target: Pakistan-facing evidence is mandatory only to the extent required by PSQCA, NEPRA / DISCO, project, tender, buyer, insurer, financier, customs, or commissioning route
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 19964-2024 and GB/T 37408-2019 are China baseline standards and may be recommended or contractually required depending on the Chinese route; they are not Pakistan approvals

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use GB/T 19964-2024 and GB/T 37408-2019 report evidence as China baseline evidence, then map them to Pakistan route-specific requirements.

source.publisher: National Public Service Platform for Standards Information / SAMR, China

rows.target_requirement: The final Pakistan file should be a concise traceability matrix, not a pile of Chinese certificates. It should link each Pakistan requirement to source evidence, responsible party, language, validity, model coverage, report edition, source URL, and open actions.
target_standards: PSQCA scope route where applicable
NEPRA / DISCO prosumer or grid-connectivity route
IEC 61000 / CISPR evidence
rows.china_equivalent: China GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024, EMC, safety, label, and factory evidence should be retained with clear limits on what each document proves and whether it matches the Pakistan SKU.
rows.gap: Use columns for Pakistan requirement, China evidence, missing evidence, source URL, edition, model coverage, owner, status, language, last verification date, and whether the item is regulatory, project-specific, buyer-requested, or due-diligence only.

mandatory_status.target: Not a regulator-mandated artifact by itself, but recommended for PSQCA, NEPRA / DISCO, buyer, tender, insurer, financier, customs, and commissioning coordination

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A traceability matrix is the clean way to reuse China baseline evidence without overstating Pakistan approval or mandatory scope.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power-Quality Evidence for Pakistan PV Inverter Files
rows.target_requirement: Pakistan-facing PV inverter files should include EMC and power-quality evidence where required by PSQCA scope, DISCO review, tender, buyer, insurer, financier, or project owner. If a Pakistan-adopted edition is not confirmed, cite the bare IEC or CISPR standard number and identify the tested edition, ports, operating modes, accessories, and model coverage separately.
target_standards: IEC 61000 series
CISPR 11 or other CISPR standard where applicable
DISCO power-quality requirements where specified
rows.china_equivalent: China EMC reports may use GB/T adoptions of IEC or CISPR standards and can support review if the tested AC port, DC PV port, communication ports, enclosure, earthing method, cable lengths, operating modes, monitoring dongles, and SKU match the Pakistan shipment.
rows.gap: Create one EMC applicability table covering emissions, immunity, surge, ESD, EFT, conducted RF, harmonics, flicker, performance criteria, test levels, tested modes, accessories, and whether each result covers the Pakistan SKU and installation environment.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless PSQCA, DISCO, buyer, tender, insurer, financier, project owner, or telecom / radio route makes the evidence required
mandatory_status.cn: China EMC evidence may support Pakistan review but is not a Pakistan EMC approval by itself

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Include EMC as a traceable project evidence set; avoid implying a confirmed whole-inverter Pakistan EMC mark unless one is actually identified.

rows.requirement: Wireless Monitoring Modules and Telecom Approval Check
rows.target_requirement: If the inverter package includes Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cellular, LoRa, or other radio modules, separate host-inverter EMC evidence from any Pakistan radio or telecom approval question for the module, data logger, antenna, label, and user instructions.
target_standards: Pakistan radio / telecom equipment approval route where applicable
IEC / CISPR EMC evidence for host inverter
rows.china_equivalent: China SRRC, CTA, CCC, CQC, or EMC evidence for a communication module may be useful background, but it does not automatically replace Pakistan radio or telecom review if the module is shipped and operated in Pakistan.
rows.gap: List every wireless module, frequency band, antenna, output power, module certificate, host integration condition, user label, and importer responsibility, then confirm whether Pakistan telecom approval is needed for the shipped configuration.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Pakistan radio / telecom equipment approval applies; otherwise project or buyer-specific
mandatory_status.cn: China radio approvals do not automatically transfer to Pakistan

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat wireless monitoring as a separate approval question from inverter EMC and grid compliance.

rows.requirement: Current NEPRA Prosumer / Net-Metering Customer Route
rows.target_requirement: For customer-side distributed solar, treat the National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Prosumer) Regulations, 2026 as the primary current route to confirm, with the 2015 Alternative & Renewable Energy Distributed Generation and Net Metering Regulations retained as historical context and amendment history. Inverter files should support the actual route selected by the Pakistan customer, DISCO, and project team.
target_standards: National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Prosumer) Regulations, 2026
NEPRA (Alternative & Renewable Energy) Distributed Generation and Net Metering Regulations, 2015
rows.china_equivalent: China rooftop or distributed-PV documents may include GB/T grid-function evidence and Chinese utility acceptance records, but they do not create a Pakistan prosumer, net-metering, DISCO, or interconnection approval.
rows.gap: Confirm whether the customer is applying as a prosumer, legacy net-metering customer, distributed generator, self-use project, or project-specific applicant; then align inverter datasheets, anti-islanding data, protection settings, single-line diagrams, meter interface details, local installer declarations, and commissioning forms to that route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for customers seeking current NEPRA / DISCO prosumer, net-metering, distributed-generation, or interconnection processing
mandatory_status.cn: China grid evidence is supporting documentation only for Pakistan customer approvals

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use the current prosumer route as the primary customer-route check; keep 2015 net-metering language as history unless the Pakistan reviewer says it still controls the case.

rows.requirement: NEPRA / DISCO Grid-Connectivity Settings and Project Evidence
rows.target_requirement: NEPRA's legal index lists 2026 technical standards for grid connectivity. For PV inverter deployments, the project file should be ready for the applicable DISCO or project route, including capacity, site type, export mode, applicant identity, voltage / frequency protection, anti-islanding, power factor, reactive-power behavior, ride-through where relevant, and commissioning records.
target_standards: National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Technical Standards for Grid Connectivity) Regulations, 2026
Applicable DISCO interconnection and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T reports can show technical capability, but Pakistani acceptance depends on the current NEPRA / DISCO route and project-specific settings, forms, and site evidence.
rows.gap: Prepare a Pakistan grid settings sheet and project matrix covering capacity, DISCO, single-line diagram, meter arrangement, protection values, firmware profile, anti-islanding test evidence, commissioning checklist, and final handover.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where current NEPRA technical standards, DISCO approval, project owner requirements, or commissioning process applies
mandatory_status.cn: China settings and utility files are not acceptable by default for Pakistan commissioning

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The Pakistan grid file must be DISCO- and project-facing; generic factory evidence should be mapped to local settings and commissioning records.

rows.requirement: PSQCA Scope Check for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Do not overclaim that standalone PV inverters are clearly PSQCA-compulsory without a product-specific citation. The honest Pakistan file should confirm, model by model, whether the inverter, bundled electrical parts, plug, adapter, enclosure, cable assembly, label, HS code, or customs route falls under a notified PSQCA compulsory conformity scheme or a buyer-required Pakistan Standard route.
target_standards: PSQCA compulsory conformity scope where specifically notified
Pakistan Standards applicable to electrical products where adopted, notified, or required by buyer / customs route
rows.china_equivalent: China factory files may include GB/T grid-tied inverter reports, CCC or CQC evidence for components, Chinese labels, and domestic shipment documents. These are useful inputs but do not determine Pakistani PSQCA scope.
rows.gap: Prepare a PSQCA due-diligence memo covering HS code, product description, AC/DC ratings, accessories, importer role, labels, customs route, and the specific official or buyer source that makes any Pakistan Standard compulsory or requested.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the exact inverter, bundled component, HS route, customs instruction, or buyer / tender document places it in a compulsory or required PSQCA / Pakistan Standard scope
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T, CCC, CQC, and domestic component approvals are supporting evidence only and do not replace Pakistan scope confirmation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat PSQCA as a scope-confirmation gate; PV inverters should not be described as clearly compulsory unless a specific Pakistan source says so for the exact product route.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence and Product Identity
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the practical international safety baseline for PV power converters and PV inverters where Pakistan stakeholders request IEC evidence. Where a Pakistan-adopted edition is unconfirmed, cite the bare IEC numbers and identify the actual lab report edition, CB certificate if any, model range, critical components, labels, manuals, warnings, importer details, and installation limits.
target_standards: IECEE CB Scheme where accepted by the stakeholder
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-performance reports and domestic safety files may not cover every IEC 62109 construction, marking, software, residual-current, bonding, insulation, and manual requirement. Use them as inputs to a crosswalk rather than as an automatic IEC safety conclusion.
rows.gap: Create one identity and safety crosswalk linking factory model codes, sales SKUs, labels, test reports, manuals, packing list, invoice, DISCO application names, IEC 62109 clauses, critical components, software version, and change-control history.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary unless PSQCA, NEPRA / DISCO, buyer, tender, insurer, financier, project owner, or customs route makes IEC 62109 or CB evidence required
mandatory_status.cn: China evidence remains separate and does not replace IEC 62109 where Pakistan stakeholders require it

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 is a safety-evidence backbone when requested, not an automatic Pakistan market approval or substitute for product identity checks.
